description: Huawei Ascend Transformer Boost (ATB) library for Transformer model inference and training acceleration on NPU

include/atb.name: Public API Headers
include/atb.description: Public API interface definitions for ATB library
security_notes: Primary external API entry point - all user inputs flow through these headers
Defines tensor structures (Tensor, TensorDesc, Dims) with user-controllable sizes
VariantPack contains input/output tensors - potential buffer overflow if sizes mismatch
GraphParam allows user-defined graph structure with up to 1024 nodes
InferShapeFunc is a user-provided callback - potential code injection
ReshapeFunc is a user-provided callback - potential code injection

src/atb/context.name: Context Management
src/atb/context.description: Context management for operation execution, stream handling, memory allocation
security_notes: Manages device/host memory allocation
Controls stream execution for NPU operations
Tiling buffer pool management - potential memory corruption
Custom allocator/deallocator support - arbitrary code execution risk
asyncTilingCopyStatus enables async copy operations

src/atb/operation.name: Operation Implementation
src/atb/operation.description: Core operation implementation including Setup, Execute, InferShape
security_notes: Execute() processes user-provided VariantPack with tensors
Setup() calculates workspace size based on user input
workspace allocation uses user-influenced size - potential integer overflow
InferShape() validates tensor dimensions - bounds check required
VariantPack.inTensors/outTensors sizes user-controlled
Graph operation with up to 1024 nodes - complex validation needed

src/atb/runner.name: Runner/Dispatcher
src/atb/runner.description: Operation dispatchers including HCCL runner, ACLNN runner, graph runner
security_notes: HCCL runner handles distributed communication - network input
ACLNN runner interfaces with CANN ACLNN library - external dependency
Graph runner executes multi-node operations - validation complexity
RunnerVariantPack contains tensor data - buffer handling risks

src/atb/utils.name: Utilities
src/atb/utils.description: Utility functions for memory, config, tensor operations, communication
security_notes: Config reads multiple environment variables (getenv)
ATB_HOME_PATH, ATB_WORKSPACE_MEM_ALLOC_ALG_TYPE, etc.
MAX_ENV_STRING_LEN check at 12800 - potential truncation
comm.cpp handles HCCL root info via shared memory
Shared memory operations with semaphore locking - race conditions
disk_util.cpp for file operations
Memory allocation solver with multiple algorithms

src/atb/kernel_cache.name: Kernel Cache
security_notes: Kernel caching on disk - potential cache poisoning
Local and global cache configuration via env vars

src/torch_atb.name: PyTorch Bindings
src/torch_atb.description: Python bindings for ATB library via pybind11
security_notes: Direct Python interface - arbitrary input from Python code
pybind11 bindings expose all operation parameters
OperationWrapper accepts Python tensors - no validation
GraphBuilder allows arbitrary graph construction from Python
set_buffer_size accepts arbitrary uint64_t - integer overflow risk
Memory manager controls workspace buffer sizes
Python can manipulate tensor shapes, types, and memory addresses

src/torch_atb/resource.name: Memory Manager
security_notes: MemoryManager::SetBufferSize controls workspace allocation
Buffer device management for NPU memory
Potential for memory exhaustion attacks

src/ops/ops_infer.name: Inference Operations
security_notes: 71 different operation implementations - validation complexity
Attention operations process user-controlled tensor dimensions
Communication ops (all_gather, all_reduce) handle distributed data
KV cache operations with memory management
Quantization operations with user-controlled parameters

src/ops/ops_train.name: Training Operations
security_notes: Training-specific operations for gradient computation
Backward pass operations with additional validation needs

src/ops/ops_common.name: Common Operations
src/ops/ops_common.description: Shared operation utilities and event operations

src/kernels/mixkernels.name: Mixed Kernel Operations
security_notes: AscendC kernel implementations - direct hardware access
Tiling calculations based on user input dimensions
Memory operations within kernels - bounds checking critical
Fused operations combine multiple ops - validation complexity

src/kernels/kernels.name: Base Kernels
security_notes: Base kernel implementations for fundamental operations
Dynamic quantization kernels with user-controlled parameters

src/kernels/lcal.name: LCAL Communication Layer
security_notes: Socket communication in tools/socket/
Collective communication kernels (AllGather, AllReduce)
LCCL (Lightweight Collective Communication Library)
Network data handling - input validation critical
Profiling and timing with external synchronization

src/kernels/tbe_adapter.name: TBE Adapter
src/kernels/tbe_adapter.description: Tensor Boost Engine adapter and platform abstraction

torch_atb.name: Python Package Entry
torch_atb.description: Python package initialization and library loading
security_notes: Loads shared libraries via ctypes.CDLL with RTLD_GLOBAL
Sets ATB_HOME_PATH environment variable
Library loading failures raise RuntimeError
Dynamic library path from package_dir - path traversal risk

scripts.name: Build Scripts
security_notes: build_util.py - build configuration
update_tbe_tactic_json.py - JSON file manipulation
build_attribute_utils.py - attribute handling

primary_entry_points.name: Python API
primary_entry_points.description: Python bindings expose all operations and parameters to arbitrary user input

primary_entry_points.name: Public C++ API
primary_entry_points.description: C++ headers define entry points for tensor operations

primary_entry_points.name: HCCL Communication
primary_entry_points.description: Distributed communication receives data from other nodes

primary_entry_points.name: Shared Memory IPC
primary_entry_points.description: HCCL root info exchanged via shared memory

data_flow_paths.description: User tensors from Python -> Operation execution
User graph definition -> Graph operation creation

high_value_targets.name: Workspace memory
high_value_targets.description: Workspace allocated based on user-influenced size

high_value_targets.name: Graph structure
high_value_targets.description: User-defined operation graph with up to 1024 nodes
